info.title: IQ Fluence API
info.description:
## Introduction
IQ Fluence API provides access to the audience demographic and psychographic attributes of Instagram, YouTube and TikTok influencers based on their active audience and social media presence.
We currently support user lookups by user ID or username for Instagram, YouTube and TikTok.

## Error codes
In case of error the API returns the HTTP status code different from 200 (4xx or 5xx code) and response is formatted as such:
```
{
    "success": false,
    "error": "code",
    "error_message": "message"
}
```

Possible values for the `error` field are bellow.

HTTP status code 400:

| Code | Comment |
| --- | --- |
| bad_request | Request body contains invalid parameters. `message` field contains details. |
| account_not_found | There are no such account on social media network, may be it has been removed. |
| account_data_removed | Information has been removed at the request of the account holder. |
| account_removed | Account has been removed from social media network. |
| account_is_private | The user has made his account private. |
| empty_audience | We have no audience for this account. |
| retry_later | We are updating audience on this account right now. Try again in 5-10 minutes. |
| bad_filter | Bad filter value. May be returned in the Search or AD |
| comment_unavailable | Comments you are trying to reach do not exist. |
| media_not_found | Media you are trying to reach does not exist. |
| entity_not_found | Entity (account, video, etc.) you are trying to reach does not exist. |
| entity_is_private | Cannot access requested entity due to access restrictions (like private or geo/age restricted posts). |
| no_tokens_remaining | There are no enough credits to process the request. |
| daily_tokens_limit_exceeded | Your daily credits spending limit exceeded. The start and end of the day are in UTC. |

HTTP status code 401:

| Code | Comment |
| --- | --- |
| not_authenticated | You haven't provided API Key with the request. |

HTTP status code 403:

| Code | Comment |
| --- | --- |
| invalid_api_key | API Key you provided is invalid. |
| permission_denied | You don't have permission for this operation. |
| token_is_disabled | Your account has been disabled for some reason. Ask support for details. |
| tokens_expired | Your credits are expired. Ask your manager for details. |

HTTP status code 404:

| Code | Comment |
| --- | --- |
| not_found | URL you have called does not exists. |

HTTP status code 405:

| Code | Comment |
| --- | --- |
| method_not_allowed | A wrong HTTP method was used. Most often it's the use of GET on endpoint accepting POST only |

HTTP status code 420:

| Code | Comment |
| --- | --- |
| conn_limit_exceeded | You have more than 100 HTTP connections opened concurrently (1000 for raw API endpoints). |

HTTP status code 429:

| Code | Comment |
| --- | --- |
| rate_limit_exceeded | Subscription requests rate limit exceeded. |

HTTP status code 500:

| Code | Comment |
| --- | --- |
| internal_server_error | Something goes wrong while processing your request. Try again later. |

get.description: Returns the current balance of API credits (tokens) and expiration date if applicable.
Get brand logo image file.

List of all interests and brands that can be used as a filter in Search or may be returned in the Audience Data report. We recommend that you cache this dictionary because it updates rarely. You can always write to us to update this dictionary with new brands of your choosing.

Instagram Only.

List all supported languages ordered by descending of their frequency. They can be used as a filter in Search or may be returned in the Audience Data report. We recommend that you cache this dictionary because it updates rarely.
Returns relevant topic tags for the query like in the relevance [search filter](#operation/search-for-influencers).

parameters.name: q
parameters.description:
Search query. Use `#` symbol for hashtags and `@` for usernames, don't forget to encode them properly as a part of URI.

Examples: `q=%23prank` to search for `#prank`, `q=%40instagram` to search for `@instagram`.

For YouTube you can use channel ID instead of username, i.e. `@UCBR8-60-B28hp2BmDPdntcQ` for the official YouTube's channel.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Limits the maximum number of results

get.description: Autocomplete users who are available under audience lookalike or relevance filters or just social media accounts.

parameters.name: q
parameters.description: Search query

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Limits the maximum number of results

get.description: All your data exports sorted by added_at field in descending order

Get contact details for specific user

### $ Paid request

Cost is 0.04 credits per every success response.

parameters.name: url
parameters.description: Influencer username, userId for link to user's profile page

post.description:
Order new data export from our search engine.
Use ```dry_run=true``` parameter to estimate the number of available accounts matching certain filters.

Information in export file includes:
* basic influencer info and highlights for specified filters,
* contact details,
* influencer demographics (gender,age,geo location).

There are no limits for data export - you can export any amount of influencers until you have enough amount of credits.

### $ Paid request

This request holds credits on your account until the report will be ready.
Holden amount calculated as follows 0.04 credits per every exported account.

Once the report is ready, we charge credits for the real amount of gathered accounts with the same unit price.

Call this endpoint with dry_run=true to estimate costs before ordering a new data export.

get.description: Get information about single specific data export.

parameters.name: report_id
parameters.description: ID of the data export you received from [New Data Export](#operation/new-data-export) operation

get.description: Download data export file in on of specified formats - GZIPed CSV or JSON, XLSX.

parameters.name: export_id
parameters.description: ID of the report you received from [New Data Export](#operation/new-data-export) operation

204.description: Returns if your export is not ready

get.description:

To get whole list just set limit=50000, there are about 32k geo locations in the dictionary.

post.description: You can create no more than 1000 custom lists per account. Ask support if you need more.

parameters.description: Skip specified amount of users (pagination).

parameters.description: Limit number of returned results. Maximum value is 10,000

delete.description:
Remove users from the list.
Pass list of users to delete inside `user_ids` request body parameter.

Pass `full=true` query parameter to remove list completely (only for "custom" lists type).
Once deleted it is not shown in the list of lists and you can't get it contents.

parameters.name: full
parameters.description: Pass true to remove list completely

post.description:
For each email provided, find one or multiple social media accounts on Instagram, YouTube and TikTok who mentioned this email in their bio or as their contact email.

### $ Paid request

Cost is 0.04 credits per every matched email.

get.description:
Returns Instagram audio feed starting from the latest one.

Every successfully made request costs 0.02 credits.

parameters.name: after
parameters.description: Pass content of end_cursor field from the last response to get next page

We need some time to fix it. If you have any questions or need further assistance, please feel free to contact us. We appreciate your patience and understanding.

get.description:
Returns TikTok challenge (tag) information by ID or hashtag.

If `url` is numeric - it's treated as challengeId.
If it contains link like `https://www.tiktok.com/tag/sometag`, then `sometag` is extracted as challenge hashtag.
Otherwise, the whole `url` value is treated as challenge hashtag.

In the case of the numeric hashtag, pass it as a URL, e.g. `https://www.tiktok.com/tag/42` for `#42` challenge.

### $ Paid request

Every successfully made request costs 0.02 credits.

parameters.name: url
parameters.description: TikTok challengeId or link to challenge's page or challenge hashtag.

get.description:
Returns YouTube channel information by ID.

### $ Paid request

Every successfully made request costs 0.02 credits.

parameters.name: url
parameters.description: YouTube channel ID either itself or within a link to channel page (like this one https://www.youtube.com/channel/UCxxxx).You can pass username (or the link with username) instead but it is less preferable way.

get.description:
Retrieve a feed of playlists for a YouTube channel by URL or ID passed to `url`.

### $ Paid request

Every successfully made request costs 0.02 credits.

parameters.name: url
parameters.description: YouTube channel ID either itself or within a link to channel page (like this one https://www.youtube.com/channel/UCxxxx). You can pass username (or the link with username) instead but it is less preferable way.

parameters.name: sorted_by
parameters.description: Sorting option: `dd` (date added) or `lad` (last video added).

parameters.name: after
parameters.description: Pass the content of the `cursor` field from the last response to get the next page.

200.description: Successful response containing playlists related to the channel.

400.description: Invalid input (e.g., missing identifier or invalid `sorted_by`).

500.description: Server error or issue with the request.

parameters.name: after

post.description:
Retrieve a feed of videos associated with a specific hashtag. Supports pagination and allows filtering by tabs such as 'all' or 'shorts'.

To obtain the next page, pass the content of the `cursor` field to the `after` query parameter.

### $ Paid request

Every successfully made request costs 0.02 credits.

parameters.name: url
parameters.description: YouTube hashtag url (e.g., https://www.youtube.com/hashtag/example)

parameters.name: after
parameters.description: Pass the content of the `cursor` field from the last response to get the next page

parameters.name: tab
parameters.description: Filter the feed by tab. Available options: `all` (default), `shorts`

200.description: Successful response containing videos related to the hashtag.

500.description: Server error or issue with the request.

get.description:
List Reports history

We keep your reports for 60 days, after report would be removed from our storage. Removed reports are not returned in this endpoint.

parameters.name: q
parameters.description: Filters reports either username starting with or full name contains input

parameters.name: url
parameters.description: Influencer username, userId for link to user's profile page.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: No more than 100

post.description:
Creates new audience data report.

It is also possible to call this endpoint with *GET* method instead *POST* to simplify testing.

We strongly recommend to use influencer's user_id rather than username (or link) wherever possible,
as Influencers often change their usernames. User ID, on the other hand, is a constant for a given user,
so it would help to avoid unexpected behaviour on report update.

You can fetch this report later by report_info.report_id in different formats (JSON, PDF) using [fetch report endpoint](#operation/fetch-ad-report).
Your report will be eliminated after 60 days of storage.

### $ Paid request

If `dry_run` query parameter is set to `true` then nothing is charged in any cases.

This request cost 1 credit for each report returned.
We charge only for successful requests and do not if there is no Report returned.

parameters.name: url
parameters.description: Username, userId or link to user's profile page.

parameters.name: ignore_removed
parameters.description: If you receive account_removed error we probably have old audience data for that account. To get it set this parameter to true

parameters.name: ignore_outdated
parameters.description: If you receive retry_later error we probably have an outdated information for that account. To get the report anyway set this parameter to true

parameters.name: ignore_empty_audience
parameters.description: If you want to ignore empty audience set this argument.

parameters.name: dry_run
parameters.description: Set to "true" or "1" to check whether the report can be built or not

get.description:
Fetch previously generated report in JSON or PDF format.

We keep old reports for 30 days.
is_stored flag in [List Reports endpoint](#operation/list-ad-reports) is indicator of is it can be retrieved or not.
If report has been removed than this endpoint return 404 error (not_found).

post.description:
Search for influencers matching certain filters.

### $ Paid request (credits)
In case query parameter `auto_unhide` is set to `1` this request may charge credits for every hidden search result according [Unlock influencers operation](#operation/unhide-influencers) pricing.
The response contains extra fields: `cost` - how much credits deducted from your balance and `shown_accounts` - list of unlocked accounts.

Without this parameter, you must manually call Unlock operation for hidden results.

parameters.name: auto_unhide
parameters.description: Automatically do paid unlock operation for all hidden search results.

post.description:
Show information for influencers hidden in search results.

### $ Paid request
Unlock of every valid search result ID for influencers previously not unlocked cost 0.02 credits.
You will see these influencers for free in all search results and data exports without contact details under any filters within the next 30 days.

credits.title: Credits
credits.description: Number of API credits (tokens) remaining in your account

expires_at.title: Expires at
expires_at.description: Expiration date of the credits, if applicable

count.title: Count
count.description: Frequency of influencers related to this language

distance.title: Distance
distance.description: The lesser is number - the closer this tag is to queried topic

freq.title: Freq
freq.description: The frequency of the tag on social media network - a bigger number means more frequency

formatted_value.title: Formatted value
formatted_value.description: Ready to use URL to contact. For example, link to youtube channel.

checked.title: Checked
checked.description: Whether it's a mutual contact (i.e. the social network account by this URL has link to the account you've requested AD on)

FilterAudienceAge.description: At least one of the provided ranges should be more than the specified percentage.

FilterAudienceBrand.description: No more than 20 values. If multiple values provided, then the user must be matched to at least one of them.

FilterAudienceGeo.description: No more than 20 values. If multiple values provided, then the user must be matched to at least one of them.

id.title: Id
id.description: You can get a valid ID from geos dictionary

code.title: Code
code.description: You can get a valid language code from our dictionary

FilterAudienceRelevance.description: Has lookalike audience as of specified influencer. For example "@topgear" means "Has audience lookalike audience of @topgear account". Lookalike means that audience follows accounts with the same topic as an audience of specified account.

FilterEngagementsRange.description: We round engagements value in the filter: whenever it’s below 500, we round it to the nearest hundred (412 -> 400; 480-> 500); when it’s between 500 and 1000, we round it to the nearest 5,00 (700 -> 500; 800 -> 1000); when it’s over 1k, we rount it to the nearest 1,000 (5400 -> 5k; 5500 -> 6k)

FilterViewsRange.description: We round views value in the filter to the nearest thousand (1400 -> 1k; 1500-> 2k).

FilterReelsPlaysRange.description: We round reels plays value in the filter to the nearest thousand (1400 -> 1k; 1500-> 2k).

FilterSharesRange.description: We round shares value in the filter: whenever it’s below 500, we round it to the nearest hundred (412 -> 400; 480-> 500); when it’s between 500 and 1000, we round it to the nearest 5,00 (700 -> 500; 800 -> 1000); when it’s over 1k, we rount it to the nearest 1,000 (5400 -> 5k; 5500 -> 6k)

FilterSavesRange.description: We round saves value in the filter: whenever it’s below 500, we round it to the nearest hundred (412 -> 400; 480-> 500); when it’s between 500 and 1000, we round it to the nearest 5,00 (700 -> 500; 800 -> 1000); when it’s over 1k, we rount it to the nearest 1,000 (5400 -> 5k; 5500 -> 6k)

FilterFollowersRange.description: We round followers value in the filter: whenever it’s below 5k, we round it to the nearest thousand (4123 -> 4000; 4800-> 5000); when it’s over 5k, we round it to the nearest 5,000 (27k -> 25k; 28k -> 30k).

FilterGeo.description: No more than 20 values. If multiple values provided, then the user must be matched to at least one of them.

id.title: Id
id.description: You can get a valid ID from geos dictionary

code.title: Code
code.description: You can get a valid language code from our dictionary

FilterRelevance.description: Filter by topic of influencers posts of by similarity of topic to other influencer. For example "#cars #audi @topgear" means "Writing about #cars and #audi, has similar topic as @topgear account"

threshold.title: Threshold
threshold.description: Threshold on relevance distance. You can view distance on search results while searching by relevance.

FilterTextTag.description: No more than 20 values.

type.title: Type
type.description: Mention or hashtag means how the value has been used in the influencer's post. To specify where the mention has been used - in the text or on the photo, use `text_mention` or `photo_mention` type.

value.title: Value
value.description: Hashtag or mention to search for, without `@` or `#`

action.title: Action
action.description: Include only (`must`, `should`) or exclude (`not`) influencers from search results. Evaluation: must && must && (should || should) && !not && !not

action.description: Include only (`must`, `should`) or exclude (`not`) influencers from search results. Evaluation: must && must && (should || should) && !not && !not

FilterSearchActions.description: Change filters mixing logic from AND to OR or NOT. For example, search for 'Followers > 1m AND NOT from United States', or 'Female OR having interest Beauty'.

filter.title: Filter
filter.description: If `keywords` or `relevance` specified then you can not sort by this field

action.title: Action
action.description: Evaluation of all filters specified in the request: must && must && (should || should) && !not && !not. By default they all are `must`, can be switched here to `should` or `not`

audience_age.description: At least one of the provided ranges should be more than the specified percentage.

audience_brand.description: No more than 20 values. If multiple values provided, then the user must be matched to at least one of them.

audience_brand_category.description: No more than 20 values. If multiple values provided, then the user must be matched to at least one of them.

audience_geo.description: No more than 20 values. If multiple values provided, then the user must be matched to at least one of them.

geo.description: No more than 20 values. If multiple values provided, then the user must be matched to at least one of them.

text.title: Text
text.description: Search phrase within influencer BIO or full name

keywords.title: Keywords
keywords.description: Search phrase influencers use in their posts texts. Search phrase influencers use when speaking in their videos.

text_tags.description: No more than 20 values.

is_hidden.title: Is hidden
is_hidden.description: Shows only private (=true) or public (=false) accounts.

is_verified.title: Is verified
is_verified.description: Shows only verified if `true` value passed.

account_type.description: Filter by account types, 1 - Regular, 2 - Business, 3 - Creator. For example, to get business or creator accounts pass [2,3] value to this filter.

audience_credibility.title: Audience credibility
audience_credibility.description: Minimum audience credibility for audience of the influencer

audience_credibility_class.description: Filter by audience_credibility class. Pass one of or multiple values - ['bad', 'low', 'normal', 'high', 'best']

filter_ids.description: Filter by specified list of user IDs, up to 1000 IDs. Use in combination with `actions` filter to exclude accounts from the result

is_official_artist.title: Is official artist
is_official_artist.description: Exclude(=false) or show only (=true) channels with Official artist label

last_posted.title: Last posted
last_posted.description: Maximum number of days since last post of influencer

actions.description: Change filters mixing logic from AND to OR or NOT. For example, search for 'Followers > 1m AND NOT from United States', or 'Female OR having interest Beauty'.

post_type.title: Post type
post_type.description: Filters posts by type. Possible values: - `all` - `all`, `videos`, `shorts`, `streams`.

id.title: Id
id.description: Affects only for sort by audience_geo, audience_brand or audience_brand_category fields to specify most audience segment to sort by according to your filter

dry_run.title: Dry run
dry_run.description: Calculate costs instead of real data export

send_email.title: Send email

name.title: Name

account_type.title: Account type
account_type.description: Instagram account types, 1 - regular, 2 - business, 3 - creator

custom_name.title: Custom name
custom_name.description: The part of YouTube Custom URL (e.g. `https://www.youtube.com/c/CustomName`) in case if account owner had set it up.

code.title: Code
code.description: Age range in format `{min age}-{max age}`, e.g. `13-44` or `45-64`.  `{max age}` will be omitted if `right_number` is not specified, e.g. `45-` matches all the audience of age 45+.

weight.title: Weight
weight.description: The percentage of audience interested in. We cut off brands with a value less than 0.005 (0.5%)

affinity.title: Affinity
affinity.description: Measure of how volume of influencer's audience interested in specific brand relates to average on Instagram. 1.0 means the same as on average, 2.0 means 2 times more than usual on Instagram.

weight.title: Weight
weight.description: We cut off countries with a value less than 0.01 (1%) but no less than 7 countries

weight.title: Weight
weight.description: We cut off US states with a value less than 0.005 (0.5%) but no less than 7 US states

weight.title: Weight
weight.description: We cut off cities with a value less than 0.005 (0.5%) but no less than 7 cities

weight.title: Weight
weight.description: The percentage of audience interested in. We cut off interests with a value less than 0.015 (1.5%)

name.title: Name
name.description: Name of the language in English

weight.title: Weight
weight.description: We cut off languages with a value less than 0.01 (1%) but no less than 7 language

title.title: Title
title.description: Name of geo location with country name, e.g. "Paris, France". For country it is equal to `name`, for cities in US - `name, state, country

remove_at.title: Remove at
remove_at.description: If not null then this account is marked for removal but not actually removed from the list. It be will be in severals seconds. It rare cases in can take several minutes.

user_ids.description: No more than 10000 IDs at once

playAddr.title: Playaddr
playAddr.description: It is often not possible to download the content using this URL directly. Use [TikTok Media Video Content endpoint](#operation/tt-media-video) instead to download the video content.

itemCommentStatus.title: Itemcommentstatus
itemCommentStatus.description: Value `3` means that comments for this post are turned off by the author. [Comments](#operation/tt-comments) endpoint returns `unavailable` error for such post.

type.title: Type

shortcode.title: Shortcode
shortcode.description: For `type=TIKTOK_VM`, `type=TIKTOK_VT` and `type=TIKTOK_WWWT`. Rarely, shortlink can lead to another shortlink. Contains shortcode of the target URL, e.g. `type=TIKTOK_VM` and `shortcode=AbCd` means current shortlink leads to `https://vm.tiktok.com/AbCd/`

location.title: Location
location.description: For `type=OTHER`, `type=TIKTOK_VM`, `type=TIKTOK_VT` and `type=TIKTOK_WWWT`. Contains URL where current shortlink leads to. VM/VT/WWWT are explained above. Links with `type=OTHER` usually lead to promotion or info pages, not related to any specific entity (post / user / etc.)

itemCommentStatus.title: Itemcommentstatus
itemCommentStatus.description: Value `3` means that comments for this post are turned off by the author. [Comments](#operation/tt-comments) endpoint returns `unavailable` error for such post.

after.title: After
after.description: For pagination pass content of ```cursor``` field from the last response to get next page.

tags.name: Authentication
tags.description: Authentication is handled via API Keys. You must send it either in the ```X-Api-Key``` header or in the ```api_key``` query string parameter in any request to our API. To obtain an API Key go to the API section on our website and click on "Generate Key" button.

tags.name: Integration
tags.description: ## Rate limiting We limit one user to make no more than 10 requests per second to any endpoint. Scraping API has different limitations - see documentation for the specific endpoint. If you send more requests you get 429 http response status code. ## Testing This section includes test usernames and other information to make sure your integration works as planned. Use it to trigger different flows in your integration and ensure they are handled accordingly. In order for requests to use features from this section make sure that you have chosen 'Test Mode' scope in your API Key. You can check it at the API section on our website. Also, if the parameter used for tests does not match those presented from the list, the request will be in a regularly mode - with billing enabled, return of the real result, etc. ### Report Test making a [new Report](#operation/new-report) You can use `url` query parameter to raise error from list of [Error codes](#section/Error-codes). Other query parameters will be ignored. In order to get the error from the endpoint you need to prefix the error code with `api_test_`, e.g. `api_test_internal_server_error` for `{"error": "internal_server_error"}` result. Example request url: POST `/v2.0/api/reports/new/?url=api_test_internal_server_error` The list of common test usernames available for testing: * `api_test_internal_server_error` * `api_test_not_found` * `api_test_rate_limit_exceeded` * `api_test_invalid_api_key` * `api_test_token_is_disabled` * `api_test_tokens_expired` * `api_test_permission_denied` * `api_test_not_authenticated` * `api_test_no_tokens_remaining` * `api_test_no_quota_remaining` * `api_test_subscription_expired` * `api_test_subscription_required` * `api_test_daily_tokens_limit_exceeded` Test usernames specific for this endpoint: * `api_test_bad_filter` * `api_test_account_not_found` * `api_test_account_data_removed` * `api_test_account_removed` * `api_test_empty_audience_data` * `api_test_retry_later_few_minutes` - we are updating data for this account, try again in a few minutes * `api_test_retry_later_n_minutes` - we are updating data for this account, try again in a specified number of minutes In case input matches the `api_test_` pattern but does not matches test usersnames above, the next response will be returned (http status code 400): ``` { "success": false, "error": "invalid_test_error_code", "message": "unknown test error code: `api_test_`" } ```

tags.name: Dictionaries
tags.description: We use various objects to look for Influencers or show metrics in Reports. For objects like brands, country, city, and languages we have a set of helper dictionaries. Requests to those dictionaries are free.

tags.name: Lists
tags.description: You can create custom lists of influencers and use them for including/excluding in [Discovery](#operation/search-for-influencers) API (using filter.lists parameter).

tags.name: Instagram Scraping API
tags.description:
Here are endpoints to get information on influencer's profile directly from Instagram website.
We do scraping in realtime and return information to you.

Sample use cases:
1. Detecting when influencer made a new post
2. Tracking performance of individual social media posts
3. Tracking influencers performance on your schedule (e.g., daily follower growth history)
4. Showing most fresh data in the UI

For everyone with valid credits we give base package for free - API is limited to 5 RPS and 0.02 credits per successful* request.
Contact your manager for subscription options.

* Successful means we got some non-500 response from TikTok. For example, if you have passed an invalid keyword and TikTok returned empty results list, then it counts as one request. If there are some problems on our side, then you would not be charged.

3. Tracking influencers performance on your schedule (e.g., daily follower growth history)
4. Showing most fresh data in the UI

For everyone with valid credits we give base package for free - API is limited to 5 RPS and 0.02 credits per successful* request.
Few endpoints costs more, it's stated in their description.
Contact your manager for subscription options.

* Successful means we got some non-500 response from TikTok. For example, if you have passed an invalid keyword and TikTok returned empty results list, then it counts as one request. If there are some problems on our side, then you would not be charged.
